description: Wire shell, service_manager, and daemon into mk_entry.cpp. Add a persistent Telegram polling daemon loop that runs in a background thread and processes incoming messages through the AI pipeline. Also wire the GENERATE route to gracefully fall back through the composer instead of requiring neural_net weights.
steps: Add #include directives in mk_entry.cpp for: ../system/daemon.cpp, ../system/shell.cpp, ../system/service_manager.cpp, ../plugins/telegram.cpp, ../ai_core/neural_net.cpp. Place telegram.cpp after the network includes.

Add member instances to MKSystem: MKDaemon daemon; MK_Shell::MKShell shell; MK_Services::ServiceManager serviceManager; MKTelegram* telegram (pointer, only initialized if token is available); MKNeuralNet neuralNet;

In MKSystem constructor: check if MK_TELEGRAM_TOKEN env var is set. If so, create a new MKTelegram instance with that token. Otherwise set telegram to nullptr and print a note that Telegram integration is disabled.

Create a telegram_poll_loop function that: (a) calls telegram->getUpdates() with offset tracking, (b) parses the JSON response to extract message text, chat_id, and update_id, (c) routes each message text through handle_natural_query logic (or a simplified version), (d) sends the response back via telegram->sendMessage(), (e) sleeps 2 seconds between polls. Use basic string parsing for JSON (the project doesn't use a JSON library). Track last update_id to avoid processing duplicates.
In main(), after system initialization and before the REPL loop, if sys.telegram is not null, spawn a std::thread running the telegram polling loop. Store the thread handle.
Register the Telegram polling as a daemon job via sys.daemon.addJob("telegram_poll", 2, ...).
Add a /shell command to the REPL that processes the remainder of the input through sys.shell.execute() and prints the result.
Add a /services command to the REPL that lists all registered services and their status via sys.serviceManager.
Register key modules as services in the service manager at boot time (e.g., 'knowledge_graph', 'reasoning_engine', 'telegram_bot') and start them all.

For the GENERATE route in handle_natural_query: The current implementation already uses the composer as fallback. Add a comment noting that neural_net.cpp is wired but has no trained weights - the GENERATE route gracefully falls back to the MKComposer. Initialize neuralNet with a tiny config (vocab=256, dim=32, layers=1, ff=64) so it exists but mark it as 'untrained' - do NOT call it in the hot path.
In the graceful shutdown, if telegram thread is running, signal stop and join the thread.
acceptance_criteria: mk_entry.cpp includes daemon.cpp, shell.cpp, service_manager.cpp, telegram.cpp, neural_net.cpp
Telegram polling runs in a background thread when MK_TELEGRAM_TOKEN is set
The polling loop tracks update_id offset to avoid duplicate message processing
The /shell command routes input through MK_Shell and prints output
The GENERATE route works gracefully without neural net weights (composer fallback)
The neural net is initialized but marked as untrained and not called in production paths
findings: Build succeeds with 11 warnings (all pre-existing or trivial: unused params, field reorder in neural_net.cpp tensors, unused lambda captures in shell.cpp). New warnings from daemon.cpp (unused crashCount field) and neural_net.cpp (field initialization order) are cosmetic. The telegram polling uses basic string parsing since there is no JSON library. The getUpdates method was extended with an offset parameter for proper update tracking. The neural net is initialized tiny (vocab=256, dim=32, 1 layer, ff=64) so it allocates minimal memory but proves the architecture is wired.
